2604.16060.title: Chain-of-Thought Degrades Visual Spatial Reasoning Capabilities of Multimodal LLMs
2604.16060.paper_summary: Multimodal Reasoning Models (MRMs) leveraging Chain-of-Thought (CoT) based thinking have revolutionized mathematical and logical problem-solving. However, we show that this paradigm struggles with generalized spatial intelligence. We perform a comprehensive evaluation of seventeen models across thirteen spatial benchmarks and identify a critical gap: CoT prompting consistently degrades performance in visual spatial reasoning. Furthermore, through a novel No-Image++ ablation, we demonstrate that MRMs and CoT prompted MLMs suffer from severe shortcut learning, and hallucinate visual details from textual priors even when the image is absent. These findings challenge the efficacy of text-only CoT for spatial tasks and underscore the need for vision-centric reasoning paradigms.
2604.16060.paper_summary_zh: 多模態推理模型（MRMs）利用基於思考鏈（CoT）的思維方式，徹底改變了數學和邏輯問題的解決方式。<lb/>然而，我們顯示這一範式在一般化空間智能方面存在困難。<lb/>我們對十七個模型在十三個空間基準上的表現進行了全面評估，並識別出一個關鍵的缺口：CoT 提示在視覺空間推理中持續降低性能。<lb/>此外，通過一個新穎的 No-Image++ 消融實驗，我們證明 MRMs 和 CoT 提示的 MLMs 受到嚴重的捷徑學習影響，並在圖像缺失的情況下，從文本先驗中幻覺出視覺細節。<lb/>這些發現挑戰了僅依賴文本的 CoT 在空間任務中的有效性，並強調了以視覺為中心的推理範式的必要性。
2604.16060.author: Sai Srinivas Kancheti et.al.
2604.16060.authors: Sai Srinivas Kancheti, Aditya Sanjiv Kanade, Vineeth N. Balasubramanian, Tanuja Ganu
